columns.extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is Docetaxel. This is explicitly stated in the title of the article: "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone...". It is also mentioned in the abstract under "Design, setting, and participants" (page 1, Chunk 4): "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients...". Further confirmation is found in the "Patients and methods" section (page 2, Chunk 4): "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone."
